title: Fritz Companion App
summary: A companion app that works the way Fritz talks to his second-in-command right now: ask a question, get an answer, run the operation — from his pocket. Mobile-first chat client over the Shay gateway, surfacing the Command Center daily briefing, with push so Shay can reach out first. This is the 'phone companion' already named in the Shay master plan Phase 3.
design_intent: Mirror the interaction model of this very session (a chat with a capable second-in-command that can act), not a generic chatbot. The app is a thin client; Shay is the brain. Shares the reach fabric with shay-omnipresent-assistant.
open_questions: Stack: PWA (fastest, installable, cross-device) vs React Native (native push/widgets) vs reuse the existing shay-desktop Electron shell.
Auth + transport between the app and the Shay gateway when off-LAN (the master plan flags API_SERVER_KEY before any exposure).
What Shay may send proactively vs what waits for Fritz.
known_gaps: Shay gateway is local today; remote/secure access for a phone off-LAN is unbuilt (master plan: set API_SERVER_KEY before Hetzner exposure).
No push pipeline yet — depends on the reach fabric from shay-omnipresent-assistant.
